item.title: 2026 Emmy nominations are announced: See the full list
item.description: The nominations for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards were announced Wednesday morning in Los Angeles. The 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards will be broadcast live on NBC on Sunday, Sept. 14.

item.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire over, "going to hit them hard again tonight"
item.description: Oil prices surge as President Trump says the ceasefire with Iran is over and the U.S. will "hit them hard again tonight."

item.title: Nolan Wells' family seeks answers after missing Mississippi teen found dead
item.description: The family of Nolan Wells, who was found dead after a July 4 boat trip to Horn Island, has hired civil rights attorney Ben Crump.

item.title: Trump wraps up NATO with press conference
item.description: President Trump has made it clear the U.S. and Turkey are friends — better friends than some other NATO allies.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will grant Ukraine's request to license Patriot defense system
item.description: "A little birdie told me this, about the fact that we'll give them the right to make Patriots," President Trump told Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

item.title: Telegram used to share rape videos and drugging tips, prosecutors say
item.description: The suspects posted videos of their attacks and referred to women as "cars," sedatives as "fuel" and rape as "driving," according to court documents.

item.title: Trump says U.S. is probably going to "hit Iran hard again tonight" after saying ceasefire is over
item.description: President Trump spoke at the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, where he said the U.S. will possibly "hit Iran hard again tonight" following overnight strikes and an announcement that the ceasefire is over. CBS News' Weijia Jiang, Courtney Kealy and contributor Samantha Vinograd have more.

item.title: Missing teen Nolan Wells found dead near Mississippi island
item.description: The body of missing teen Nolan Wells was discovered off the coast of Horn Island, Mississippi, officials said. Wells was last seen with a group of friends days before.

item.title: "Ted Lasso" actor Cristo Fernández shares new children's book "Fútbol Is Life"
item.description: "Ted Lasso" actor Cristo Fernández shares the inspiration behind his new children's book "Fútbol is Life!" The bilingual book draws on Fernández's childhood experiences growing up in Guadalajara and is "a love letter to the game" of soccer.

item.title: What's behind the surge in mahjong's popularity in the U.S.?
item.description: Mahjong, a traditional Chinese tile game, is rapidly increasing in popularity across the U.S. Caitlin Huey-Burns headed to a mahjong parlor to see why the game is seeing renewed appreciation.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Kentucky governor requests update on Mitch McConnell's health
item.description: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear formally requested an update from Sen. Mitch McConnell amid a weekslong hospitalization that has prompted growing speculation about his health.

item.title: Toddler declared dead in pool accident found alive in hospital morgue
item.description: Two police officers saw possible signs of life, but the child was still taken to the hospital's "cold room" after being treated by staff, according to police documents.

item.title: Chef Antonia Lofaso helps struggling restaurants with "Kitchen Undercover"
item.description: Chef Antonia Lofaso helps struggling restaurants get back on track with her new show, "Kitchen Undercover." Lofaso joins "CBS Mornings" to explain how.

item.title: Key takeaways from the Michigan Democratic debate
item.description: Michigan Senate hopefuls, Rep. Haley Stevens and Abdul El-Sayed, faced off Tuesday night in a debate that many saw as a battle between moderate and progressive Democrats. Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Prince Harry's U.K. trip mired with family drama and Daily Mail verdict
item.description: Prince Harry's visit to the U.K. included a dismissal of his invasion of privacy lawsuit against the Daily Mail. Chris Livesay reports.

item.title: Intense debate in Michigan among Democrats vying for Senate seat
item.description: Michigan Democrats Abdul El-Sayed and Haley Stevens debated on Tuesday as the state's primary nears. CBS News' Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: USPS raising the price of a Forever stamp starting on Sunday
item.description: The cost of a first-class Forever stamp has climbed 41% since 2021, and postal officials have signaled they want prices to rise even more.

item.title: Does debt relief erase all of your debt?
item.description: Debt relief promises a fresh start, but the fine print actually determines how much debt disappears in the process.

item.title: Stocks slide, oil surges after Trump says Iran ceasefire is over
item.description: Oil prices jumped 6% while U.S. stocks fell as renewed Middle East conflict threatens crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Democrats start turning against Graham Platner
item.description: Maine Democratic leaders are now turning against Senate candidate Graham Platner as more abuse allegations emerge against him. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Ex-Bucknell coach charged in football player's death after he collapsed during practice
item.description: Former Bucknell strength and conditioning coach Mark Kulbis is facing criminal charges in the 2024 death of Calvin "CJ" Dickey Jr., who collapsed during a football practice and died. Pennsylvania prosecutors allege Kulbis knew about Dickey's sickle-cell trait, which put him at a greater risk of serious injury or death from extreme exertion. Jericka Duncan reports.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran is over: "They're scum, they're sick people"
item.description: President Trump insulted Iranian leaders during the NATO summit following a tense period of strikes and the apparent dissolution of diplomatic talks to reach a peace deal. Yossi Mekelberg, a senior consulting fellow at Chatham House, joins CBS News with more.

item.title: Trump administration's DOGE agency shutting down after controversial run that began with Elon Musk
item.description: The Department of Government and Efficiency, or DOGE, is shutting down after being a prominent feature of the Trump administration's second term. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul reports.

item.title: Half of millennials feel pressure to choose between stability and parenthood, study shows
item.description: Millennials and Gen Z are feeling the financial crush when it comes to the decision to have children, research shows. CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger has tips on managing the pressure.

item.title: Here's how Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee if Platner drops out
item.description: Democrats have a brief window of time to replace Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner on the ballot if he drops out of the race amid a sexual assault allegation.

item.title: DOJ threatens criminal action against states that allow noncitizens to vote
item.description: The head of the Justice Department's Civil Rights Division sent letters to election officials in all 50 states threatening criminal action if they knowingly allow non-U.S. citizens to vote.

item.title: China warns of "security backdoor" in Anthropic AI coding tool
item.description: A Chinese industry regulator warned users of a "security backdoor" embedded in versions of U.S. artificial intelligence company Anthropic's coding tool, Claude Code.

item.title: Why do airlines make passengers check their carry-on at the gate?
item.description: Catching a flight with just a carry-on can make travel feel easy. No need to check a bag and all your belongings stay with you. But quite often, that suddenly changes before boarding.

item.title: Ford recalls 177,000 vehicles over faulty wiper, drivetrain and other issues
item.description: A slew of Ford recalls affects some new and used Mustang, Lincoln Nautilus Hybrid and Explorer Hybrid vehicles, according to a federal safety watchdog.
